critiques.summary: I have reviewed the implementation, tests, and documentation for the per-session routed-verification gating predicate and the associated policy changes. The core logic in `ai_router/routed_gate.py` is sound and well-tested in `ai_router/tests/test_routed_gate.py`, with coverage for all specified triggers, edge cases, and the critical "overrides can only raise" invariant. The "cut-over" to this new gated policy is correctly and consistently reflected in the primary workflow document (`docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md`), the router configuration (`ai_router/router-config.yaml`), and the new strategic synthesis document (`docs/verification-surface-strategy.md`). Release artifacts (`pyproject.toml`, `ai_router/CHANGELOG.md`) are also consistent. All checked claims were substantiated by the code on disk.

I reviewed the Set 068 shipped surfaces directly from disk, including ai_router/routed_gate.py, ai_router/tests/test_routed_gate.py, ai_router/__init__.py, ai_router/router-config.yaml, docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md, docs/verification-surface-strategy.md, ai_router/docs/close-out.md, ai_router/docs/pull-verifier.md, docs/proposals/2026-06-14-verification-surface-empirics/proposal.md, the Set 068 spec/change-log, and the saved path-aware critique artifact. The routed-gate code and its test file are broadly consistent, but I found material doc drift in the workflow after the claimed cut-over and a bad saved critique artifact that records repository-false findings while the set claims the artifact was successfully produced as its required dogfood output.

findings.description: The workflow doc still asserts mandatory per-session verification in sections outside the updated Step 6, contradicting the Set 068 cut-over claim that routed verification is now gated. Ground truth: docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md still says each session 'Ends with a mandatory cross-provider verification' and its standalone 'Cross-Provider Verification' section says 'Every session ends with an independent verification step', while the same file's overview and 'Verification-surface policy' sections say Set 068 demoted this to a routed_gate-based gated check. This leaves the canonical workflow internally inconsistent about current behavior. Fix: update the stale mandatory-language sections to reflect the gated predicate, and explicitly distinguish routed per-session verification from the always-run end-of-set path-aware critique / contract gate.

The checked-in required dogfood artifact contains repository-false findings but is still presented as this set's saved path-aware critique output. Ground truth: docs/session-sets/068-cadence-study-and-contract-gate/path-aware-critique.json records a Google critique claiming docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md and docs/verification-surface-strategy.md are missing and that python modules are not under ai_router/, but those files and paths do exist on disk. Because the artifact is the required close-out evidence that 'the critique ran', keeping a provider entry that demonstrably misread the repository undermines the set's own verification evidence and any downstream remediation that consumes it. Fix: rerun the critique producer/manual flow from a clean repository-root sandbox and replace this artifact with the actual raw provider outputs from a correctly scoped run; do not keep a known-bad critique as the saved dogfood artifact.
